name: dbt_corpus_convergence
host_notes: Phase F closes the last 0%-success skill on dbt (multi_hop_lineage). Adds an entity_id -> ancestors precompute via outbound `dependsOn` BFS, expands name seeds with their lineage when the intent contains lineage keywords (`upstream`, `lineage`, `ancestors`, `dependencies`), and runs GraphSearch with depth=0 so the precomputed closure is the pack — not a starting point for further BFS. Also fixes a name-index priority bug surfaced during diagnosis: 'customers' was binding to the source `raw.customers` instead of the mart `model.jaffle_shop.customers`, which left the lineage expansion with nothing to expand.

eval/corpora/dbt_loader.py: build_name_index now does two-pass indexing — models first, then sources/tests. Without this, 'customers' bound to `source.jaffle_shop.raw.customers` (a leaf with no lineage to expand) and the multi_hop_lineage intent silently failed.
build_lineage_index() — at corpus-load time, computes the transitive closure of outbound `dependsOn` edges for every entity. Returns entity_id -> ordered list[ancestor entity_ids].
expand_seeds_with_lineage() — when intent contains a lineage keyword, expand each seed with its precomputed ancestors. Compose with extract_seed_ids.
code_changes_under_test.eval/scenarios/dbt_corpus_convergence/sc: _build_pack accepts the lineage_index, expands name seeds before union with category seeds, and uses depth=0 when either category OR lineage expansion contributed seeds.

skills_unblocked.multi_hop_lineage: 0.0 -> 1.0 — lineage expansion fills all 6 required upstream entities into the pack via depth=0 + precomputed closure

skills_unchanged_below_100.column_transformation: 0.8182 -> 0.8182 — pre-existing; original B-1 run also showed 11/12 success on these 11 firings. Likely a single-query phrasing issue, not a lineage / category / seed-extraction issue.

cumulative_arc_dbt.total_lift_from_baseline: +0.56 across three lifts; $0.0001 total paid spend on this corpus

interpretation.headline: Same architectural pattern as Phase E (precompute at corpus-load time, look up at query time, use depth=0 to make the precomputed result first-class). Where Phase E precomputed *category buckets* (schema='marts' -> set, test_type='not_null' -> set), Phase F precomputes *per-entity lineage closures* (m_customers -> set of all upstream Datasets). The category index is keyed on phrase; the lineage index is keyed on entity_id and looked up after name extraction.
interpretation.name_priority_was_load_bearing: Diagnosis found `customers` -> `source.jaffle_shop.raw.customers` (a leaf, no lineage to expand). The two-pass indexing (models first, then sources/tests) is what made expansion fire on the right entity. Same root cause shape as the github phase-D 'unique-only filter' tightening — the seed extractor's first attempt wasn't selective enough.
interpretation.remaining_gap: column_transformation (0.82 = 9/11 success) is the only sub-100% skill on dbt. Inspecting prior runs, the same number turned up under Phase B-1 with-GraphSearch (also 0.82), so this isn't a regression from any change in the seed/lineage stack — it's a query-shape mismatch that's been there since corpus inception. Likely the intent for one of the three column_transformation queries phrases the lookup in a way the current keyword/semantic scoring under-ranks. Out of scope for this hardening pass.

decision_recorded: Phase F closes multi_hop_lineage on dbt. Combined with Phase E (github author_attribution, dbt category skills), seven of eight dbt skills and six of seven github skills are now at 100%. The two open eval-blocked items in TODO are now: github multi_pr_series Q1 (body-token indexing or query rewriting — still open) and column_transformation on dbt (newly surfaced as the only sub-100% dbt skill — query-shape, not seed-extraction).
